conversation.text_en: Hi, I'm your therapist. Everything we discuss here is confidential, so please feel comfortable.
Thanks, I appreciate that.
That's great to hear the traffic was manageable. So, have you been in therapy before or is this your first time seeking help?
No, never been in therapy before. Honestly, I just need some guidance on how to manage my work life a bit better, you know? School counseling can be overwhelming.

I completely understand. As a school counselor yourself, it's great that you're recognizing the importance of self-care and seeking help. What's been going on at work that's got you feeling overwhelmed?
Well, we've had some new staff members start recently and it feels like nobody knows what they're doing. It's stressful trying to keep up with everything.
That sounds really challenging. Have you noticed any changes in your daily routine or habits as a result of this stress?
Yeah, I mean, I'm not sleeping as well as I used to and my interest in hobbies has been kind of waning lately.
Sorry to hear that sleep has been affected. How's your work-life balance been overall?
It's been tough to separate the two, honestly. I find myself thinking about work even when I'm not working, which is probably part of the problem.
That makes sense. Can you tell me more about what's keeping you up at night or worrying you during the day?
Yeah, just all the little things that are adding up – missed deadlines, forgotten appointments... it feels like my brain is always racing.
It sounds like your mind is constantly busy with work-related tasks. Have you noticed any changes in your physical sensations or body feelings as a result of this stress?
Oh, yeah! I get these weird tingles sometimes, and my shoulders are all tight from being tense.
Okay, that makes sense. It's great that you're acknowledging those physical sensations. How do you usually handle stressful situations like this?
Um... I mean, I try to just power through, but it doesn't always work out so well.
That can be really tough. Have you thought about what self-care practices might help you feel more relaxed and in control?
Honestly? Not really. I should probably make some time for that, though.
Absolutely! Let's explore some ideas together. What are some things you enjoy doing in your free time?
Well, I love hiking and reading... when I have the chance.
Those sound like great hobbies to get back into. Have you had any major life changes or stressful events recently that might be contributing to these feelings?
Yeah, my cat passed away a few months ago, and it's been tough adjusting without her.
I'm so sorry to hear that. Losing a pet can be really difficult. How have you been coping with that loss?
It's been okay... I mean, there are days when it feels like the wound is still open, but I try to stay focused on work and other things.
That sounds like a really healthy approach. What are some of your goals for our therapy sessions? Are you hoping to improve your relationships, find more balance in your life?
Yeah, all those things. Just feeling like myself again would be great.
Well, we can definitely work on that. Let's make a plan to prioritize self-care and coping strategies. What do you think about setting aside time each day for yourself?
That sounds doable... I guess I just need to remember to take care of myself.
Absolutely! Remembering to prioritize your own needs is a big step. And finally, what are some things that bring you joy or make you feel fulfilled outside of work?
Hmm... probably spending time with friends and family, doing my favorite hobbies...
Those sound like wonderful sources of comfort and fulfillment. Let's keep exploring ways to incorporate those into your daily routine.
Sounds good to me!
Great! Let's wrap up our session today by setting a goal for you to practice self-care this week. How about taking 10 minutes each morning to meditate or stretch?
That sounds reasonable, yeah.
Excellent! I'll send you some resources on meditation and relaxation techniques after the session. And don't forget, everything we discussed here is confidential.
Got it. Thanks for being understanding.
You're welcome! We'll continue to work through this together next time. Take care of yourself!
Will do, thanks!
